title: cephaelis utakwae
intro: Cephaelis utakwae, also known as the Jamaican Dogwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The Jamaican Dogwood has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its active compounds, including a unique alkaloid called utamine. The health benefits of Cephaelis utakwae include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as potential treatment for anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
